metric_value: Organizations on threshold of EC revolution
notes: Aberdeen conclusion: 'we are currently on the threshold of a revolution in Electronic Commerce'

metric_value: Question is not will it grow but how fast
notes: Aberdeen frames EC growth as certain; the analytical question is growth rate not whether growth occurs

metric_value: Rapid adoption of Internet technologies simultaneously creates opportunities and risks
notes: Aberdeen identifies dual nature of Internet-enabled EC: enormous opportunity alongside significant organizational risk

metric_value: EDI is EC predecessor being supplemented by Internet Commerce
notes: White paper explicitly addresses EDI-to-IC transition; EDI as established EC infrastructure being extended by Internet

metric_value: Understanding impact of virtual channels on business equilibrium
notes: First analytical framework objective: assess how virtual channels disrupt existing business channel equilibrium

metric_value: Assess and evaluate EC and IC supplier technologies
notes: Second framework objective: structured evaluation of EC and IC vendor technologies

metric_value: Map organizational requirements to supplier product functionality
notes: Third framework objective: requirements-to-product mapping for EC/IC procurement

metric_value: IC will grow explosively; real question is how fast
notes: Aberdeen's central prediction: Internet Commerce growth is certain and will be rapid

metric_value: Confirmed: global e-commerce exceeded $6.38T by 2024; grew from near-zero in 1997
notes: E-commerce grew from ~$500M in 1997 to $6.38 trillion projected for 2024 per eMarketer (source: The Fulfillment Lab ecommerce history 2024)

metric_value: EDI will be supplemented and eventually replaced by Internet-based EC
notes: Report's EDI-to-IC transition thesis implies progressive replacement of EDI with Internet Commerce

metric_value: Partially confirmed: EDI persists in legacy supply chains but internet-based B2B dominant
notes: EDI still used in large retailer/supplier relationships (Walmart etc.) but web EDI and API-based B2B integration became dominant for new implementations

metric_name: Virtual channels disruption of business equilibrium
metric_value: Virtual channels will fundamentally alter competitive dynamics
notes: Aberdeen predicts virtual channels will disrupt existing business equilibrium across industries

metric_value: Confirmed: virtual channels disrupted retail (Amazon), travel (Expedia), media, financial services
notes: Amazon disrupted retail; Expedia and Priceline disrupted travel; Netflix disrupted media — all exactly the virtual channel disruption Aberdeen foresaw

metric_value: EC creates impacts on both internal and external business processes
notes: Aberdeen identifies dual organizational impact: internal process transformation and external partner/channel relationships
